author_claims.claim_text: Chronic lesion environments can become permissive to axon growth when scar/graft architecture supports regeneration.
author_claims.notes: Seed claim; full-text extraction required before saturation-level consensus.

author_claims.claim_text: Homologous neural grafts can enable robust corticospinal regeneration in selected experimental contexts.
author_claims.notes: Seed claim; full-text extraction required before saturation-level consensus.

author_claims.claim_text: Graft-enabled regeneration should be cross-tagged with Module 4 rather than treated as scaffold-free intrinsic regeneration.
author_claims.notes: Seed claim; full-text extraction required before saturation-level consensus.

author_claims.claim_text: Pericyte-derived scar attenuation is a candidate route to CST/RST regeneration and recovery.
author_claims.notes: Seed claim; full-text extraction required before saturation-level consensus.

author_claims.claim_text: CSPG-receptor pathway manipulation can release corticospinal growth constraints after SCI.
author_claims.notes: Seed claim; full-text extraction required before saturation-level consensus.

author_claims.claim_text: Extrinsic inhibition should be separated into scar-cell, matrix, and receptor mechanisms.
author_claims.notes: Seed claim; full-text extraction required before saturation-level consensus.

author_claims.claim_text: Lesion-remote astrocyte control of microglia-mediated white matter repair links tract repair to immune-glial regulation.
author_claims.notes: Seed claim; full-text extraction required before saturation-level consensus.

author_claims.claim_text: Human motoneuron and ventral-root pathology should constrain animal remyelination and axon-repair translation.
author_claims.notes: Seed claim; full-text extraction required before saturation-level consensus.

author_claims.claim_text: Cellular energetics is a plausible intrinsic-growth intervention axis for axonal regeneration.
author_claims.notes: Seed claim; full-text extraction required before saturation-level consensus.

author_claims.claim_text: Adult injured neurons may re-enter embryonic-like transcriptional growth states.
author_claims.notes: Seed claim; full-text extraction required before saturation-level consensus.

author_claims.claim_text: Intrinsic growth competence should be curated separately from environmental permissiveness.
author_claims.notes: Seed claim; full-text extraction required before saturation-level consensus.

author_claims.claim_text: MAP2K activation or HF-rTMS can be framed as an activity/signaling-enabled sprouting-regeneration strategy.
author_claims.notes: Seed claim; full-text extraction required before saturation-level consensus.

author_claims.claim_text: Sprouting, regeneration, and functional regeneration require distinct labels in Module 5.
author_claims.notes: Seed claim; full-text extraction required before saturation-level consensus.

author_claims.claim_text: Module 5B currently supports a seed mechanism map, not saturation-level consensus.
author_claims.notes: Seed claim; full-text extraction required before saturation-level consensus.

boundary_records.interpretation_rule: CST/RST sprouting, tract regeneration, and functional regeneration need separate labels.
Graft-enabled axon growth overlaps with Module 4 transplantation and should not be read as cell-free regeneration.
Scar, CSPG receptor, metabolic, transcriptional, and stimulation strategies test different repair axes.
White matter repair may depend on remote astrocyte-microglia programs, linking Module 5 to Module 3.
Human motoneuron and ventral-root pathology should constrain animal white-matter repair claims.
Module 5A has not reached saturation and has not completed experiment-level extraction.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Seed evidence supports graft- or permissive-context-enabled axon regeneration, especially in chronic scar/graft and homologous neural graft settings.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Seed evidence supports graft- or permissive-context-enabled axon regeneration, especially in chronic scar/graft and homologous neural graft settings.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Scar attenuation and CSPG-receptor pathway manipulation are distinct extrinsic routes for releasing CST/RST growth constraints.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Scar attenuation and CSPG-receptor pathway manipulation are distinct extrinsic routes for releasing CST/RST growth constraints.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: White matter repair may be governed by lesion-remote astrocyte and microglia interactions, making immune-glial state part of axon repair synthesis.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: White matter repair may be governed by lesion-remote astrocyte and microglia interactions, making immune-glial state part of axon repair synthesis.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Human motoneuron and ventral-root pathology should remain a translation checkpoint for remyelination and axon-repair claims.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Human motoneuron and ventral-root pathology should remain a translation checkpoint for remyelination and axon-repair claims.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Intrinsic neuronal growth competence includes metabolic and transcriptional state changes that should be curated separately from environmental permissiveness.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Intrinsic neuronal growth competence includes metabolic and transcriptional state changes that should be curated separately from environmental permissiveness.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Module 5B should keep sprouting, regeneration, and functional regeneration separated until saturation and experiment-level extraction are complete.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Module 5B should keep sprouting, regeneration, and functional regeneration separated until saturation and experiment-level extraction are complete.
